title: rubus × cuspidiformis
intro: Rubus × cuspidiformis, also known as the Himalayan blackberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, characterized by its thorny stems and dark purple berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and ellagic acid. The health benefits of Rubus × cuspidiformis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and dietary supplements.
